channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukrainians disappointed after Trump-Zelenskyy meeting fails to immediately secure Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainians shared their disappointment Saturday that the U.S. may not provide Kyiv with long-range Tomahawk missiles, while work to repair the damaged power supply to the country's Zaporizhzhia power plant soothed other concerns surrounding Europe's largest nuclear plant.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The most recent torrential rains in east-central Mexico, which have left at least 76 dead and dozens more missing, have raised questions again about the government’s ability to alert people to severe weather in time.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US Embassy issues warning to Americans in Trinidad and Tobago as tensions with Venezuela escalate
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Trinidad and Tobago is cautioning Americans to stay away from U.S. government facilities on the twin-island nation.
item.dc:creator: Anselm Gibbs, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After air strikes, peace talks between Afghanistan and Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD/KABUL, Oct 18 (Reuters) - Afghanistan and Pakistan were holding peace talks in Doha on Saturday, both sides said, after the South Asia neighbours extended a ceasefire following a week of fierce border clashes.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Yunus Yawar, Asif Shahzad, NewsNation

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: Britain believes its experience in Northern Ireland could help achieve a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui And Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Trump says it is not 'easy' for US to give Tomahawk missiles to Zelensky, Ukraine
item.description: President Trump during a White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said it is not “easy” for the U.S. to give long-range Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine, as Washington needs the powerful munitions in its stockpile also.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Russia convicts 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges
item.description: A court in southern Russia convicted 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges Friday after a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thatcher centennial celebration examines how Reagan partnership guides modern US-UK relations
item.description: Experts examine how the Cold War alliance between President Ronald Reagan and Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher influences today's U.S.-U.K. relationship amid Russian President Vladimir Putin's threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hannibal Gadhafi, son of late Libyan leader, ordered released in Lebanon if he pays $11 million bail
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A Lebanese judge on Friday ordered the release of the son of Libya’s late leader Moammar Gadhafi on condition that he pay $11 million bail. Hannibal Gadhafi has been imprisoned in Lebanon for a decade without being charged.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Child watching surgery allegedly drilled into patient’s skull
item.description: The unnamed physician denies the accusations but admits to bringing her daughter into an operating room at Graz Regional Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Greene rebukes Trump administration’s multibillion-dollar bailout for Argentina
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) sharply criticized the Trump administration on Thursday for seeking to provide as much as $40 billion in financing for Argentina to help the Latin American ally stabilize its turbulent financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Russia targets Ukraine energy facilities ahead of Trump meeting with Zelensky
item.description: Russia hit Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles Thursday, a day ahead of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s meeting with President Trump at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Putin plan Hungary meeting on Ukraine war, Zelenskyy to visit White House Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump schedules call with Russian President Vladimir Putin as Ukraine seeks long-range Tomahawk missiles following recent Russian strikes on energy sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Just-released hostage attends funeral of fellow soldier, whose body was among few returned from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Two hostages released by Hamas were reunited Wednesday in a Jerusalem cemetery for a final goodbye. Surrounded by hundreds of mourners, Matan Angrest, who had returned to Israel just two days earlier, stood before the freshly dug grave cradling his 22-year-old commander, Capt.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida doctor detained by Israel during aid mission to Gaza describes experience
item.description: Two Florida residents who were detained by Israel during an aid mission to Gaza are describing their experience in a Wednesday press conference.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: NATO allies agree to increase weapon deliveries to Ukraine
item.description: Over half of NATO allies are now part of the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program to buy U.S. weapons for the Eastern European nation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: US blasts China's expansion of rare earth export controls
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Top U.S. officials on Wednesday blasted China's major expansion of rare earth export controls as a threat to global supply chains, but said Beijing could still change course and avoid steps by Washington to decouple from the world's second-largest economy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Lawder and Andrea Shalal, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pakistan and Taliban agree to 48-hour ceasefire after renewed fighting kills dozens
item.description: A temporary ceasefire has been agreed to after border violence escalated between Pakistan and Afghanistan over the weekend as both sides blame each other for attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at some world leaders who have fled uprisings
item.description: A military coup this week in Madagascar forced the president into exile, joining a list of international leaders who fled their countries or went into hiding during uprisings to avoid incarceration or retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Gumede And Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Parents of 2 US citizens demand return of their sons’ bodies and all deceased hostages held by Hamas in Gaza
item.description: Two American citizens, Itay Chen and Omer Neutra, remain among deceased hostages held by Hamas as terror group violates hostage exchange agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia makes more precise drones and is using them to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Russian drones smashed into the Shostka train station in northeastern Ukraine earlier this month, they killed a 71-year-old man, injured at least eight people and left train cars buckled by fire and riddled with shrapnel holes.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese airlines protest US plan to ban their flights over Russian airspace
item.description: The U.S. side has said such flights give Chinese airlines an unfair cost advantage over American carriers, which cannot cross through Russian airspace.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian aerial attack hits a Ukrainian hospital, days before Zelenskyy meets Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched powerful glide bombs and drones against Ukraine’s second-largest city in overnight attacks, hitting a hospital and wounding seven people, an official said Tuesday, as European military aid for Kyiv dropped sharply and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensk
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel says it has received the remains of 4 more deceased hostages as cuts to aid for Gaza loom
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas hastened Tuesday to ease the pressure on a fragile ceasefire in its war with Israel by returning the bodies of more dead hostages.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Therapist explains what Hamas hostages are feeling
item.description: Dr. Rebecca Bailey joins "Banfield" to discuss the mental process the former Hamas hostages will be going through.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli hostages returning home need space to adjust, father says
item.description: "People need to realize that the hostages that we know so well for two years are not a public property," says Adi Alexander.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners freed as Trump tours region
item.description: Hamas released all 20 of the last living hostages on Monday, the Israeli military said, as part of a breakthrough ceasefire after two years of war between Israel and Hamas in the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: How the US brokered the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas saw the release of Israeli hostages and Palestinian prisoners and detainees.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza photojournalist says his disabled brother is missing
item.description: A Gaza-based freelance photojournalist said his disabled brother went missing in August after seeking aid. The family hoped he would be returned today.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation
